title: Placeholder Rendering Cleanup
objective: Eliminate unresolved project-level placeholders from generated docs while preserving only slice-local placeholders where a human still needs to fill data in.
description: Several generated docs still contain tokens such as `[project]`, `[project-name]`, and `[project-slug]`, which makes the output look unfinished and confuses first-time adopters. This slice standardizes project-scoped placeholders on the `{{PROJECT_*}}` contract and ensures `init-docs.sh` renders them consistently.
must: Replace project-scoped placeholder tokens such as `[project]`, `[project-name]`, and `[project-slug]` with renderable template variables across docs and templates
Ensure `init-docs.sh` renders those project-scoped tokens in every generated file, including the generated `pr.md.template`
Leave only the slice-local placeholders that are intentionally filled by the human during implementation planning
not_included: Changing the slice-local authoring placeholders inside `slice.json` beyond project-scoped references
acceptance: A generated project contains no unresolved project-scoped placeholders under `docs/` or `specs/<project-slug>/`
The generated `pr.md.template` references the concrete project slug instead of `[project-slug]`
Only slice-local placeholders such as title, objective, or case descriptions remain where user input is still required
tests: Generated project placeholder scan for project-scoped tokens
assumptions: Project-scoped tokens should be rendered during initialization, not left for manual cleanup
Slice-local placeholders remain useful as planning prompts
